title: Jessie Buckley Wins Best Actress Drama at Golden Globes - Hollywood Reporter
text: Jessie Buckley claimed the Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Motion Picture Drama on Sunday for her role in Hamnet. During her acceptance speech, Buckley expressed gratitude to the film's cast and crew, saying I adore you. I would rave in that globe for the rest of my life with you, referencing the 400 extras. Buckley highlighted the film's international scope, noting that while the story centered on Shakespeare, we had a Chinese director, a lot of Irish and mostly Polish crew. This victory follows Buckley's recent Critics Choice Award win and Screen Actors Guild nomination. Hamnet had an exceptional night at the ceremony, earning six nominations total, including Best Picture Drama, which served as the evening's final award.
